perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, neither party shall, without the prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit the employment of any employee of such other party, unless such employee seeks employment on an unsolicited basis or in response to general solicitations or advertising or third party employment agencies, provided that such general solicitations or advertising is made, taken out and distributed in the ordinary course and not in an effort to specifically target employees of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, neither party shall, without the prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit the employment of any employee of such other party, unless such employee seeks employment on an unsolicited basis or in response to general solicitations or advertising or third party employment agencies, provided that such general solicitations or advertising is made, taken out and distributed in the ordinary course and not in an effort to specifically target employees of the other party. **However, this clause does not restrict either party from hiring employees who independently leave their current employment.**
perturbation.explanation: The added clause creates an in-text contradiction. While the original text restricts soliciting employees, the added clause allows hiring those who leave independently. This creates ambiguity about whether actively recruiting employees who have already left their position is still prohibited, undermining the original intent of the non-solicitation agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, neither party shall, without the prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit the employment of any employee of such other party, unless such employee seeks employment on an unsolicited basis or in response to general solicitations or advertising or third party employment agencies, provided that such general solicitations or advertising is made, taken out and distributed in the ordinary course and not in an effort to specifically target employees of the other party. However, this clause does not restrict either party from hiring employees who independently leave their current employment.
perturbation.justification: The 'Changed Text' adds a clause stating that the non-solicitation agreement does not restrict hiring employees who independently leave their current employment. This directly contradicts the original text, which broadly prohibits soliciting the employment of any employee of the other party. The added clause creates ambiguity about whether actively recruiting employees who have already left their position is still prohibited. Because the contradicted text and changed text are in the same location, it creates a strong, clear contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any Transaction involving the parties shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered by the parties. Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. For purposes of this paragraph, the term "definitive agreement" does not include an executed letter of intent or any other preliminary written agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any Transaction involving the parties shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered by the parties. Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. For purposes of this paragraph, the term "definitive agreement" **includes an executed letter of intent provided both parties have legal counsel present at the signing.**
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of 'definitive agreement' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly excludes letters of intent, while the changed text includes them under specific conditions. This introduces uncertainty about when a binding agreement exists, contradicting the intent of having a clear threshold for contractual obligation. This contradicts the intent of the original text, creating ambiguity on when a contract comes into existence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any Transaction involving the parties shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered by the parties. Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. For purposes of this paragraph, the term "definitive agreement" includes an executed letter of intent provided both parties have legal counsel present at the signing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'definitive agreement' to include letters of intent under specific conditions. The original text explicitly excludes letters of intent. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This creates a clear contradiction because the definition of 'definitive agreement' is now inconsistent within the contract itself. The contract now both includes and excludes letters of intent, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces uncertainty on whether a letter of intent constitutes a definitive agreement under specific circumstances. This fits the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction"

perturbation.original_text: Except as explicitly provided in sections (8) and (9) above, this Agreement shall terminate one (1) year from the date first set forth above.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as explicitly provided in sections (8) and (9) above, this Agreement shall terminate one (1) year from the date first set forth above. **However, this agreement will continue to be in effect until the transaction is complete.**
perturbation.explanation: The added clause creates an in-text contradiction regarding the termination date of the agreement. The original clause states the agreement terminates one year from the initial date, while the added clause states it continues until the transaction is complete. This creates uncertainty about when the obligations under the agreement cease, potentially leading to disputes about confidentiality and non-use obligations after the one-year mark.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as explicitly provided in sections (8) and (9) above, this Agreement shall terminate one (1) year from the date first set forth above. However, this agreement will continue to be in effect until the transaction is complete.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating the agreement continues until the transaction is complete, which directly contradicts the original clause stating the agreement terminates one year from the initial date. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the termination date, making it an in-text contradiction as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition: "Inconsistencies also lead to **in-text contradictions** when **different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties**."

perturbation.original_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that either party or any of its Representatives have breached this Agreement, then the breaching party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the reasonable legal fees 4 incurred in connection with such litigation, including an appeal therefrom. In no event shall either party be liable for consequential or punitive damages.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that either party or any of its Representatives have breached this Agreement, then the breaching party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the reasonable legal fees 4 incurred in connection with such litigation, including an appeal therefrom. **In the event of gross negligence, punitive damages may be awarded.** In no event shall either party be liable for consequential or punitive damages.
perturbation.explanation: The introduced sentence creates an in-text contradiction by stating that punitive damages *may* be awarded in cases of gross negligence, directly contradicting the subsequent sentence that says "In no event shall either party be liable for consequential or punitive damages." This creates uncertainty about whether punitive damages are recoverable, creating an in-text contradiction within the same text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that either party or any of its Representatives have breached this Agreement, then the breaching party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the reasonable legal fees 4 incurred in connection with such litigation, including an appeal therefrom. In the event of gross negligence, punitive damages may be awarded. In no event shall either party be liable for consequential or punitive damages.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that punitive damages *may* be awarded in cases of gross negligence, directly contradicting the subsequent sentence that says "In no event shall either party be liable for consequential or punitive damages." This creates uncertainty about whether punitive damages are recoverable, creating an in-text contradiction within the same text. The contradicted text is in the same location, which enforces that this is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. **However, parties are not responsible if a Representative breaches the contract after they are no longer employed by the party.**
perturbation.explanation: This added sentence creates an in-text contradiction. The original text holds each party responsible for any breach by their representatives, while the addition releases them from responsibility if the breach occurs after the representative is no longer employed. This creates an ambiguity regarding the extent of liability, and undermines the original clause's intent to have accountability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. However, parties are not responsible if a Representative breaches the contract after they are no longer employed by the party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original statement that each party is responsible for any breach by their representatives. The added sentence states that parties are not responsible if a representative breaches the contract after they are no longer employed. This directly contradicts the original obligation and creates an in-text contradiction because the first sentence originally placed full responsibility on the party, and the added sentence limits that responsibility depending on the employment status of the representative, leading to uncertainty and unenforceability. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies leading to in-text contradictions because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
